creator: tz@freebsd.org
text:
(In reply to lightside from comment #8)

> It's a following command, just for other people who curious:
> % cd tuxpack && patch -B '' < ../tuxpuck.diff

Okay, i phrased this poorly. :D I meant that i asked how to handle patch-files which just changes the generation date. I personally see no purpose in committing changes without any effect and would have excluded them. 
But after talking to my mentors the conclusion is, that if there are changes with effects (like your Makefile-changes) it is totally fine to pet portlint.
